name: Taj Mahal
description: UNESCO ivory-white marble mausoleum on the south bank of the Yamuna river.

name: Agra Fort
description: Historic fort in the city of Agra in India, the main residence of the Mughals.

name: Mehtab Bagh
description: Charbagh complex in Agra, north of the Taj Mahal and Agra Fort.

name: Charminar
description: Mosque and monument, known as the 'Arc de Triomphe of the East'.

name: Golconda Fort
description: Fortified citadel and an early capital city of the Qutb Shahi dynasty.

name: Salar Jung Museum
description: Art museum located at Dar-ul-Shifa, on the southern bank of the Musi River.

name: Hawa Mahal
description: Palace of Winds, built from red and pink sandstone.

name: Amer Fort
description: Magnificent fort known for its artistic Hindu style elements.

name: City Palace
description: Palatial complex in Jaipur, including Chandra Mahal and Mubarak Mahal.

name: Gateway of India
description: Arch-monument built during the 20th century in Bombay.

name: Marine Drive
description: 3.6-kilometre-long Promenade along the Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose Road.

name: Elephanta Caves
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site with temple caves dedicated to Lord Shiva.

name: Red Fort
description: Historic fort in the city of Delhi that served as the residence of the Mughal Emperors.

name: India Gate
description: War memorial located astride the Rajpath, on the eastern edge of New Delhi.

name: Qutub Minar
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site, a 73-metre minaret that forms part of the Qutb complex.
